ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-17-2023 Continued To Aug-23-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Alan Silver appearing via zoom for Wendace Witt (petitioner).
Denise Chambliss appearing via zoom for Wendace Witt (petitioner).

Mr. Silver addressed his non-appearance stating that he was in a major car accident in December and that he was unable to get things done. The Court finds good cause. The order to appear/show cause is ordered off calendar. Mr. Silver also stated that he was drafting a petition, but got notice of the substitution of attorney and that he will forward the paperwork to Mrs. Chambliss. Mrs. Chambliss requested 3 months to get up to speed. Discussion was held regarding the taxes and need for a corrected inventory and appraisal. Status on closing of the estate is continued to August 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The pleadings are to be filed by August 16, 2023.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Either Antoher Petition To Approve Status Of Administration, Or Petition For Final Distribution.
Off Calendar May-18-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alan Silver, in court, for and with Wendace Witt, Executor; Mitchell Greenberg, via Zoom, for Exchange Bank, Creditor.

The court and Mr. Silver discuss the status of the estate and whether it is ready to be closed, and if not, why no petition to approve a new status report has been filed. Mr. Silver reports of having a Petition to Approve the Status of Administration which he intends to file as soon as the hearing is done. The court reminds him that the petition is required to be electronically filed. Mr. Silver advises that he failed to appear at the last calling of this matter due to an emergency family issue. Mr. Silver reports as to his client's plans to refinance a real property to pay a pending estate tax but is waiting for a pending reappraisal to determine whether the tax payment will be lower than expected. Mr. Greenberg reports that he has not seen or heard from the Executor regarding financials to support her plans and that the bank would like to see either a reassignment of the borrower's note or the confirmation of a loan to pay the bank's creditor's claim. Mr. Silver reports that talks are ongoing with other banks regarding refinancing and is open to include Exchange Bank in those discussions.

After additional discussion, the Order to Appear is taken off calendar and based on Mr. Silver's statement that he will be filing a petition to approve status of administration, the Status Hearing is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

***After Hearing: upon further consideration, A Status on Close of Estate is set for Jun-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mitchell Greenberg, via Zoom, for Exchange Bank, Claimant.

The court notes that there is no appearance by Executor or her attorney. Mr. Greenberg reports of receiving an email from Alan Silver last evening advising that he would not be appearing today. Mr. Greenberg offers a status regarding his client's position.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Executor Wendace Witt and Attorney Alan J. Silver to appear on May-18-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. An Order to Show Cause why Alan J, Silver should not be sanctioned $250 for failure to appear is also set for May-18-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Obitinalla. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Either Antoher Petition To Approve Status Of Administration, Or Petition For Final Distribution.
Off Calendar Feb-09-2022 Continued To Apr-20-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alan Silver appeared by zoom for Wendace Witt.
Mitchell Greenberg appeared by zoom for Exchange Bank.

The court asked Mr. Silver what has transpired in the last two months. Mr. Silver explained that he had some medical issues, that he plans to have a petition filed within 30 to 45-days, and that he will be picking up the files within 4 or 5-days. Mr. Greenberg stated that the reason exchange bank is here is because they have a loan on a piece of property that the estate owns, that they have been waiting for the financials from the executor so that they could attempt to process a refinance, that they have been waiting for 2 to 3-years and that they need to get those financials to move this forward. Status on close of estate is continued to April 20, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-11-2020 Continued To May-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Howard Olsen for Wendace Witt.
Mitchell Greenberg appeared by telephone for Exchange Bank.

The Court raised the issue of the estate tax obligation which is to be paid over a period of ten years, questions how the estate could be closed with something pending for that length of time, and noted that there is a creditor's claim from Exchange Bank which has not been allowed or rejected. Mr. Olsen stated that his Client is exploring another method of paying the tax obligation by taking out a loan against the residential property in Menlo Park, that they believe the loan can be funded in 30 to 45-days and that the plan is to still have the Petitioner assume the loan, or refinance and pay off Exchange Bank. The Court once again questions how this estate can be closed with these two issues still pending and stated that this is a premature petition. Mr. Olsen requested a 60 to 90-day continuance and stated that his Client and her Financial Advisor have set up what they consider a workable plan. Mr. Greenberg stated that they are ready, willing and able to process the loan, but they have been waiting to receive the papers for processing. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to May 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Olsen that either an amended petition that resolves the two issues needs to be filed, or this petition will go off calendar and needs to be replaced with a petition for approval of a report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-09-2019 Continued To Mar-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Howard Olsen for Wendace Witt.
Mitchell Greenberg via Court Call for Exchange Bank.

Mr. Olsen stated he has not been able to file the amended return, the tax bill of $3.8 million dollars has not been paid, they are securing a loan in place by February 2020 to pay those taxes and request a continuance to March 2020. Mr. Greenberg stated they are willing to allow Ms. Witt to substitute in to take over assignment, under the terms of the loan it is in default when the person died, and they are waiting for appraisal.

The Accounting Petition re: Final Distribution on Waiver of Account is continued to March 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: T. Jackson. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Continued To Oct-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Howard Olsen for Wendace Witt (Petitioner).

The Court noted that Mr. Olsen filed a declaration with the inventory and appraisal attached, that there is only one beneficiary and questions why the estate can't be closed. Mr. Olsen stated that the Referee signed the inventory and appraisal, that the Client has not signed it, that he is meeting with his Client this week, and that there are no real issues as to why the estate can't be closed other than paying property taxes and obtaining a final letter from the Internal Revenue Service. The Court informed Counsel that it will not extend administration of the estate for the requested one year period, but it will grant a four month extension. The Report on Status of Administration and Request for Extension of Time is continued to October 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for February 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Howard Olsen for Petitioner.

Mr. Olsen informed the Court that he filed a report on status of administration with a hearing date of October 3, 2018 and a partial inventory and appraisal on July 16th.
Status on close of estate is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Continued To Jul-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Howard Olsen for Wendace Witt.

The Court informed Counsel that an inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution is needed. Mr. Olsen stated that they have not made any progress, that the Client is incommunicado, that the Client was overwhelmed because she was hit with a bigger tax bill and that she is going to sell one of the properties. Status on close of estate is continued to July 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that if he is not ready to proceed with final distribution, he will need to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration to obtain permission for continued administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Saelee. Not reported.
